codeReview.summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature metadata, worker handoff, transcript skeleton, worker skill, and the full diff for commit 91c9dcb8df9d66192a9f15f0e82b6ebc24822200. The implementation converts 14 chained .filter().map() data-transformation paths into single .flatMap() iterations across 14 files, exceeding the feature's 7+ target while preserving the feature description's readability guidance. The conversions are semantically equivalent, including tuple construction for Map inputs in generate-certificate-pdf.ts and single-pass field mapping in the field helpers. I also checked the current tree for remaining .filter().map() chains and only found JSX-rendering cases that the worker intentionally and appropriately left unchanged. Build and lint were already verified in the handoff, and I found no regression in the reviewed scope.

issues.description: This diff includes unrelated Tailwind class reordering alongside the intended flatMap refactor. The change is harmless but adds review noise and is not part of VAL-JS-002.
This diff includes unrelated Tailwind class reordering in TooltipContent. It is functionally harmless but unrelated to the iteration refactor.
This diff includes unrelated Tailwind class reordering in TooltipContent. It is functionally harmless but unrelated to the iteration refactor.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature wording says '7+ instances' but the worker found and converted 14 eligible data-transformation chains after broader searching. The mission/shared guidance would be clearer if it explicitly distinguished data-transformation chains from JSX-rendering .filter().map() patterns that should usually stay unchanged for readability.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The worker transcript shows multiple searches to separate eligible data paths from JSX rendering cases, and the final diff converts 14 files while leaving JSX chains such as apps/remix/app/components/dialogs/envelope-redistribute-dialog.tsx:133-134 untouched.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Workers doing narrow refactors can still pick up unrelated class-order churn from formatting. Shared guidance could recommend minimizing or reverting unrelated formatter-only changes before commit so scrutiny diffs stay focused on the requested optimization.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit 91c9dcb8df9d66192a9f15f0e82b6ebc24822200 includes Tailwind class-order changes in document-recipient-link-copy-dialog.tsx, document-global-auth-action-select.tsx, and recipient-action-auth-select.tsx that are unrelated to combining filter/map iterations.

summary: Pass. VAL-JS-002 is satisfied: 14 eligible chained .filter().map() usages were consolidated into single .flatMap() iterations without evident regression, and the remaining JSX-rendering chains were reasonably left alone for readability. Only non-blocking issue: the commit includes a few unrelated Tailwind class-order changes.
